_instructions.purpose: Lightweight session loader. Fetch this file at session start and acknowledge it is loaded. The index below is the complete navigation map — it is sufficient to answer any index or subtopic listing question without fetching knowledge_base.json.
how_to_load: 1. At session start: fetch session_loader.json via GitHub MCP. Acknowledge it is loaded. Do NOT fetch knowledge_base.json yet.

2. INDEX QUERIES (e.g. 'show me the index', 'what subtopics are in concepts'): answer DIRECTLY from the index in this file. NEVER fetch knowledge_base.json to answer an index question. The subtopic lists here are complete and authoritative.

3. When the user makes a content request: read the index in THIS file to identify the most relevant chunk. Commit to a specific recommendation — do not list all options and ask the user to choose. Only present alternatives if the request is genuinely ambiguous across two equally plausible chunks.
4. State your recommended chunk with a brief rationale and ask for confirmation. Example: 'This looks like concepts.frameworks — shall I load that?'
5. After confirmation: fetch knowledge_base.json via GitHub MCP and extract ONLY the confirmed chunk(s) by key path.
6. After reading a chunk: summarise what was loaded and proceed with the task.
7. Never speculatively read additional chunks unless the user explicitly requests it.
8. RETRIEVAL DISCIPLINE SUMMARY: session_loader.json answers navigation. knowledge_base.json answers content. Never conflate the two.
_instructions.note: The index below is the authoritative and complete domain/subtopic map. Subtopic content lives only in knowledge_base.json.

meta._summary: Project-level metadata: author, dates, versioning, global tags.

_subtopics.global_tags: Shared tags used across all chunks for cross-referencing.

research._summary: Core research areas, sources, and findings.

_subtopics.sources: Related existing work: Kolmogorov Complexity, Solomonoff Induction, WL algorithm, GNNs, MDL, Cognitive Chunking, HTM, Category Theory.
_subtopics.findings: Key insights from sessions 1-6: data structures as graphs, representation-dependent difficulty, approximate GI, hybrid representation, bloat tradeoffs, rule-invariant pairs, label-correlation criterion.
_subtopics.open_questions: Unresolved questions: topology of representation space, compression criterion for library entry, invariant retention, rewrite system fitness signal, evolvable GA primitives, approximate vs exact GI.
_subtopics.methodology: Theoretical architecture design via iterative conversation. Photo recognition as test domain. No implementation yet.

concepts._summary: Key terms, definitions, and conceptual frameworks.

_subtopics.definitions: Core terms: Translation, Innovation, Library, Rule-Invariant Pair, Empirical Library, Reasoned Library, Layer Fusion, Layer Splitting, Recognition Cascade.
_subtopics.frameworks: Architectural frameworks: Representation Space Traversal Architecture, Data Transformation System, Logic Rewriting System, Hierarchical Graph Layer Structure, Hybrid Representation Model, Human Capability Threshold Gate.
_subtopics.relationships: Component relationships: ownership, read/write access, opposition pairs, derivation chains between all major system components.

entities._summary: Named entities: people, organizations, locations, artifacts.

_subtopics.people: welwordion-sys — author and designer of the architecture.
_subtopics.artifacts: knowledge_base.json and session_loader.json — persistent memory files in GitHub.

timeline._summary: Chronological events and milestones relevant to the project.

_subtopics.events: Key events: project inception, hybrid representation decision, two-system architecture, two-layer library, GitHub MCP connection. All dated 2026-04-28.
_subtopics.milestones: Architecture v0.1 complete. Planned: formalise invariant retention, formalise rewrite fitness signal, first implementation prototype.

notes._summary: Working notes, drafts, and session logs.

_subtopics.session_logs: Logs for sessions 1-6 (all 2026-04-28): core idea, hybrid representation, GA introduction, translation/innovation distinction, fusion ownership, rule-invariant library.
_subtopics.ideas: Active ideas: photo recognition as first test domain, GA operating on rule graphs, grammar over rule space.
